payload.claim_text: Select Bus Service (SBS) is New York City's brand name for a package of improvements that result in faster and more reliable service on high-ridership bus routes.
There are seven SBS routes currently operating in NYC
SBS features include improved fare collection (off-board), bus lanes, transit signal priority, passenger information, stations and amenities, and branding.

Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street where buses operate quickly and reliably, bus customers safely and easily access bus stations, pedestrians are comfortable walking on and crossing the street, and drivers get where they need to go at a reasonable and safe speed.

Community feedback identified: bus service is unreliable/slow during rush hour, transit improvements needed especially in Rockaways, pedestrian crossings are long and dangerous, congestion leads to long trips, changing road widths/configurations make corridor difficult to navigate.

MTA Bus is currently studying community request to extend the Q52 further east. Analysis includes origin/destinations, transfers, trip generators, ridership, and Q52/Q53-Q22 Transfer Survey (March 2015).
In order to improve travel time and reliability, SBS bus stops are spaced farther apart than Local Bus Stops. In the Rockaways, the Q52/53 LTD currently makes many Local stops.

SBS stops at 91 Av instead of Atlantic Av (local bus will still stop at Atlantic Av). New stop at 101 Av. New stop at Pitkin Av. Consolidated SBS stops in Broad Channel and the Rockaways. Discontinued Q52/Q53 LTD stops will still be served by Q22 within 500-1100 ft of a proposed SBS stop.

payload.context: bus only lanes and off-board fare collection will make riding the Q52/Q53 25-35% faster

payload.description: Existing Conditions & Analysis phase of the Woodhaven/Cross Bay SBS project
3 Design Concepts & Screening phase of the Woodhaven/Cross Bay SBS project

Preferred Corridor Design phase - Develop draft corridor design plan based on chosen design concept; public design workshops and stakeholder meetings; refine draft design and bus stops through community feedback, technical analysis, and transportation goals for NYC
Final Design & Engineering phase of the Woodhaven/Cross Bay SBS project
Present draft plans at public design workshops and stakeholder meetings to get feedback; draft plans available on project website for further comment
Refine design plans based on community feedback and further technical review
Transfer project to NYC Dept. of Design and Construction for Final Design and engineering
